fields.body:
# memory-viewers

memory-viewers is the parent repository for the shared viewer stack. It contains the repo-local rule workspace that coordinates generated documentation across nested child repositories such as memory-api and viewer-api.
fields.title: memory-viewers

fields.body:
# memory-viewers

memory-viewers is the umbrella repository for the user-facing tools in this stack. Start here when you want a visual entry point into tickets and specs, or when you need generated documentation that explains how the nested repositories fit together.

![memory-viewers repository surface](.github/assets/repository-surface.svg)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    User((User)) --> TV[ticket-viewer]
    User --> SV[spec-viewer]
    User --> Docs[repo READMEs]
    TV --> TicketAPI[memory-api ticket surfaces]
    SV --> SpecAPI[memory-api spec surfaces]
    Docs --> Tooling[memory-api automation]
    Tooling --> ViewerAPI[viewer-api]
```
fields.title: memory-viewers

fields.title: memory-viewers

fields.body:
# memory-viewers

memory-viewers is the top-level repository for the user-facing viewer tools and the nested toolchain they depend on.

## Tool Surface

| Tool | What it exposes | Use it when |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ticket-viewer` | Ticket board and graph views | You want to review active work, owners, and dependency flow visually. |
| `spec-viewer` | Spec browsing UI | You want to inspect specs, sections, and linked code references. |
| `memory-api` | CLI, MCP, HTTP, and VS Code tooling | You need automation or authoring workflows behind rules, specs, tickets, and audits. |
| `viewer-api` | Shared viewer-facing APIs | You need reusable viewer integration surfaces across the stack. |
fields.title: memory-viewers
